sessions.intent: Validate test quality and run full suites for typed-action-returns branch (Stages 0-4 + Serializers Data refactor + http body dispatch)

before.assumptions: Coder reports 3123/3123 C# + 208/0/12 PLang. codeanalyzer v3 PASS. No baseline-tests.md from coder (process violation — to note).
before.risk: Branch made wide-ranging signature changes (Data<T> typings across ~10 actions, Serializers Data refactor, Ask shape change, http.Response). False-greens likely in stage tests if they only check signature shape, not behavior. PLang 12 'stale' placeholders need verification — could be real missing coverage.

actions.context: Captured full-suite runs after clean rebuild — C# 3124/3124 + PLang 221/221 PASS. PLang stale-binary trap navigated via rm bin/obj + rebuild PlangConsole + rebuild PLang.Tests.

Quality analysis: audited 13 TypedReturns plang goals (no text/action drift), Stage 0-4 C# tests (Build inference seam is strong; signature-reflection contracts are honest). Surfaced 4 minor + 1 info finding. Verdict: PASS.

Flagged missing-coverage on the Serializers Data.Fail wrapping (try/catch added by refactor, never exercised by tests) and the http TextFallback parse-failure path. Weak-assertion on FileRead BuildWarning test.

after.notes: Full suite green after clean rebuild. 4 minor + 1 info finding, none blocking. Branch is good to hand to security review.

sessions.intent: Write failing-test contract for the 5 stages of typed-action-returns (Branch A)

before.assumptions: Architect plan v3.3 + stages.md are locked. Stages 0-4 ship Branch A; Branch B is separate. Tests are signatures-only with Assert.Fail / throw 'not implemented'. C# under PLang.Tests/App/TypedReturnsTests/, PLang under Tests/TestModule/TypedReturns/.
before.risk: Build() interface + named-channels are new infra — easy to mis-shape the tests if coder's exact API names differ. Mitigation: test against documented contract surfaces (interface presence, return shape), not internal naming.

actions.context: Stage 0 foundation infra: PlangType removal (6), Build() interface (8), named channels + BuildWarning (9), Data materialization API (7). 30 C# unit signatures total.
Stage 1 tester/File → tester/Test rename. 6 C# units verify type existence, field carryover, and absence of old name everywhere.

Stage 2 mechanical typings — 10 handlers split into two batches. Each handler covered for (a) Run() signature shape, (b) Modules.Describe() catalog return-line, (c) no Data<object> double-wrap. 19 C# units total.

Stage 3 HTTP Response record (6) + runtime Content-Type body dispatch (8) — covers all four fields, the four content-type branches (json/text/binary/missing/text-fallback/csv-materializer), and that http.download is exempt.

Stage 4 per-action Build() impls (11) + (type) hint Compile rule + multi-segment GetByExtension + precedence (8). The precedence file is the trickiest: distinguishing explicit-object from default-object is called out explicitly.

12 PLang .test.goal files — the user-visible end-to-end contract. Each pins a single behaviour (trace-snapshot annotation, build warning surfacing, hint precedence, lazy materialization). Bodies are throw 'not implemented'.
Plan + verdict + summary per character-file workflow.

after.health: PLang.Tests builds clean (0 errors). 88 C# unit signatures + 12 PLang behaviour goals = 100 failing tests staged for coder.
after.notes: User approved all 11 batches in a single pass. No blockers. Recommended next bot: coder, starting at Stage 0.

sessions.intent: Review Stage 0 production code on typed-action-returns

before.assumptions: Stage 0 = 6 coder commits c4404b9c5..3c8285760: IClass interface, Build() pass in validate, named channels w/ noop, slim [PlangType], public Data.As(string), generator SetAction. Coder handoff says 30/30 Stage 0 tests green.
before.risk: Diff is small but architecturally dense — generator emission, attribute redesign, validate hook, materialization API split.

actions.context: Read each file, ran 5 passes. Findings are all LOW severity polish. Real cleanup: AllowMultiple=false vs Registry's multi-attr loop is dead code + stale doc.

after.health: green — no blocker; Stage 0 tests green per handoff; findings are polish/follow-up.
after.notes: 8 findings: 7 LOW + 1 design call (IClass.SetAction default). Most actionable: [PlangType] AllowMultiple=false contradicts Registry comment+loop.

sessions.intent: Review typed-action-returns Stages 1-4 + serializer/http bonus production code

before.assumptions: Stages 1-4 + Serializers-return-Data + http-body-dispatch landed across 15 commits (68319f649..9513a6fe7). Coder handoff says PLang.Tests 3123/3123 green, plang --test 208 pass.
before.risk: Big surface (~770 insertions across 51 production files). New: Response record, Ask record w/ ShouldExit override, file.read/llm.query/http.* Build() impls, Serializers returning Data, Schema→Types rename, MockHandle→Mock rename. Footgun: Data<object> double-wrap if forwarders mishandled. Apply new leaf-returns-Data rule.

actions.context: Five-pass + new leaf-returns-Data rule + Data<T> implicit-op footgun audit across Stages 1-4 + bonus Serializers/HTTP-body-dispatch. 11 LOW findings; none block ship. Meatiest cleanup: F3 (HTTP leaf helpers throw instead of Data) + F4 (Read{String,Bytes} duplicate).
Standard codeanalyzer artifacts. Summary rewritten to cover v2 (Stages 1-4 + bonuses).

after.health: green — no blocker; PLang.Tests 3123/3123 + plang --test 208/208 per coder handoff; all findings are polish/follow-up.
after.notes: 11 findings: 4 readability, 3 simplification, 2 behavioral, 1 leaf-Data, 1 polish carried from v1. Most actionable: HTTP leaf helpers (F3, F4).

sessions.intent: Re-review v2 follow-up commits on typed-action-returns

before.assumptions: Coder addressed F1-F7, F9-F11 across 4 commits (117b754f3..HEAD); F8 closed won't-fix by architect. PLang.Tests + plang --test green per coder handoff.
before.risk: Spill-over property rename (Mock.ActionPattern -> Pattern), precedence swap in getTypes.cs, new registered-types gate in file/read.cs each carry small behavioral risk worth re-pass.

actions.context: Five-pass + leaf-Data + Data<T> footgun on the 13-file v2-followup diff. All 10 actionable findings closed; 2 info-level observations recorded (N1 stale %__data__% comments, N2 mild warning-gate side effect).
Standard codeanalyzer artifacts; summary overwritten to cover v3.

after.health: green - branch is in shape to merge; build clean, test posture green per handoff.
after.notes: All v2 findings closed. N1 (3 stale %__data__% comments in goal/getTypes.cs) and N2 (registered-types gate also silences missing-file warning for unregistered exts) recorded as info-level only.
